text: Posterior tibial tendon dysfunction (PTTD) treatment in athletes should be guided by understanding the specific deformity components to maximize nonoperative intervention.
Orthotics and physical therapy are the primary nonoperative interventions for Stage I and II posterior tibial tendon dysfunction in runners.
Not all athletes with posterior tibial tendon dysfunction improve with nonoperative treatment (orthotics and physical therapy), and some require surgical intervention.
Return to full athletic activity after posterior tibial tendon dysfunction is not universally possible, even after surgical reconstruction.

Surgical reconstruction for Stage I and II posterior tibial tendon dysfunction can be successful in minimizing symptoms and enabling an active lifestyle, though not necessarily full return to running.
